title: 10x Is Easier Than 2x vs Who Not How: Head to Head Comparison
metaDescription: Dan Sullivan's 10x Is Easier Than 2x vs Who Not Howâ€”two Strategic Coach books compared. Which paradigm shift will transform your thinking?

book1.title: 10x Is Easier Than 2x
book1.subtitle: How World-Class Entrepreneurs Achieve More by Doing Less
book1.author: Dan Sullivan and Benjamin Hardy
book1.description: 10x Is Easier Than 2x challenges conventional thinking about growth. Dan Sullivan and Benjamin Hardy argue that going for 10x growth forces you to eliminate 80% of what you're doing and focus only on your unique ability. Incremental 2x thinking keeps you stuck doing the same things just a little better. The book shows how 10x thinking requires letting go, saying no, and building around your zone of genius. It's about quality over quantity, transformation over addition, and freedom through subtraction.

book2.title: Who Not How
book2.subtitle: The Formula to Achieve Bigger Goals Through Accelerating Teamwork
book2.author: Dan Sullivan and Benjamin Hardy
book2.description: Who Not How presents the paradigm shift from asking 'How do I achieve this?' to 'Who can help me achieve this?' Sullivan and Hardy show how trying to figure everything out yourself limits growth. By finding the right people (Whos) to handle tasks, you multiply your capabilities and free yourself for highest-value work. The book covers mindset, building relationships with Whos, and why collaboration beats solo effort. It's about leveraging other people's unique abilities while focusing on your own.

table.book2: Who can help me achieve this?

table.book1: Elimination and focus on unique ability
table.book2: Delegation and finding the right people

book1Strengths: The 80% elimination rule forces you to drop activities that don't create 10x results which sounds radical but actually works
Going for 10x feels easier because you're forced to think completely differently instead of grinding harder at incremental 2x
At 336 pages with 2,800 ratings at 4.7 stars Dan Sullivan's Strategic Coach methodology has transformed thousands of entrepreneurs
The Unique Ability concept identifies work you love doing, are excellent at, creates energy for you, and delivers massive value
Counterintuitive psychology works because shooting for 10x eliminates all incremental thinking and forces breakthrough innovation
The freedom of focus framework shows how eliminating 80% of activities creates exponentially more value with the remaining 20%
book1Weaknesses: At 2,800 ratings versus Who Not How's 5,800 ratings the 10x concept hasn't resonated as widely with readers yet
The book is abstract and philosophical with 336 pages exploring why to think 10x but much less tactical how to execute
Eliminating 80% of your work assumes you have team members to delegate to which requires privilege and existing resources
Setting 10x goals can paralyze you if you're running a  business and shooting for  feels completely impossible
The Strategic Coach language and frameworks assume you're already a successful entrepreneur not someone just starting out
book2Strengths: The Who Not How framework is instantly applicable by asking who can do this instead of how do I do this myself
At 256 pages versus 10x's 336 pages it's more concise while showing how finding Whos multiplies your impact exponentially
Practical delegation framework included showing exactly how to find Whos, empower them properly, and build accountability systems
Works at any budget level whether your Who is a  hourly virtual assistant or  hourly specialist or equity partner
Once you internalize who not how thinking you automatically stop being the bottleneck and start building real leverage
At 5,800 ratings versus 2,800 for 10x this framework has resonated more broadly with entrepreneurs at all stages
book2Weaknesses: Assumes you can afford to hire Whos which isn't realistic for bootstrapped solopreneurs burning through their savings
The delegation advice feels obvious to anyone who's managed teams before so experienced leaders won't find much new here
Some readers find it repetitive because the core concept is simple and could be explained in 50 pages not 256 pages
Less transformative than 10x for people who need the mindset shift more than they need the delegation mechanics
The examples skew toward high growth entrepreneurs so corporate employees or non profit leaders need to translate heavily

book1: 10x growth requires 10x thinking, which requires eliminating 80% of what you're currently doing.
The biggest obstacle to 10x growth is the commitment to 2x growth.
Going 10x forces you to focus only on your unique ability.
Quality over quantity. Transformation over addition.
The only way to 10x is to let go of your 80%.
2x is about addition. 10x is about transformation.
book2: Stop asking 'How do I do this?' Start asking 'Who can do this?'
Who Not How is about abundance, not scarcity.
You can have anything you want if you know the right Who.
Freedom comes from having the right Whos in your life.
The quality of your life is determined by the Whos in your life.
Your job is to find the right Whos and to be a great Who for others.

book1: You're overwhelmed doing too many things and need permission to eliminate
You want to think exponentially (10x) instead of incrementally (2x)
You need to identify your unique ability and focus there exclusively
You're ready for a transformational mindset shift about growth
You want Sullivan and Hardy's most recent thinking (2023)
book2: You're stuck doing everything yourself and want to build a team
You need a practical framework for delegation and collaboration
You want the fundamental mindset shift from 'how' to 'who'
You're ready to find and empower the right people
You want the more popular and widely-adopted Sullivan concept

verdict.summary: These books are complementary, not competing. 10x Is Easier Than 2x (2023, 2,800 ratings, 4.7 stars) teaches WHAT to focus onâ€”eliminate 80% and focus on unique ability. Who Not How (2020, 5,800 ratings, 4.6 stars) teaches HOW to delegateâ€”find Whos for everything else. Together they're complete: 10x identifies your highest value work, Who Not How shows you how to delegate the rest. Most people need bothâ€”10x for strategic clarity, Who Not How for tactical execution.
verdict.recommendation: Read Who Not How FIRSTâ€”it's shorter (256 vs 336 pages), more popular (5,800 vs 2,800 ratings), and immediately actionable. Start finding Whos today to handle tasks outside your unique ability. Then read 10x Is Easier Than 2x for the mindset shift on elimination and exponential thinking. Together they're powerful: Who Not How gets you delegating, 10x gets you eliminating. If you only read one: Who Not How for practical delegation advice, 10x for transformational thinking. Ideally, read bothâ€”they're two sides of the same coin.
